finding_refs.assertion_text: Anti-TfR/BACE1 bispecific antibodies cross the blood-brain barrier and reduce brain amyloid-beta levels in human TfR knock-in mice.
Systemic administration of a bispecific anti-TfR/BACE1 antibody leads to a greater reduction in brain amyloid-beta (Abeta) levels compared to a monospecific anti-BACE1 antibody.
A bispecific antibody targeting TfR with low affinity and BACE1 with high affinity accumulates in the mouse brain more effectively than a monospecific anti-BACE1 antibody.
BACE1 inhibitor trials failed due to mechanism-independent side effects
BACE1 has essential non-amyloid substrates that explain inhibitor toxicity
